trackingsummarydata.subject: The Iliad, musical chemistry and more. Welcome back!
trackingsummarydata.title: 01/22/2025 02:49:19 PM

urls.page_title: The Iliad in Ithaca: Re-reading the Trojan War - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
This event is part of the “Arts Unplugged” series in the College of Arts & Sciences and is open to the public.
The event will kick off Wednesday March 12 with a virtual panel discussion from 4:30-6 p.m. on eCornell, featuring translator Emily WI...

Find out your role in PMA's vibrant projects and collaborations! Mark your calendar for Projects & Participation Kick-off on Wednesday, January 22 at 7:30 p.m. in the Kiplinger Theatre, Schwartz Center for the Performing Arts.

urls.page_title: CCHK Salon: On Music and Science Inspired by Madame Lavoisier - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Cornell Center for Historical Keyboards Salon with Patricia Garcia Gil: A Salon on Music and Science Inspired by Madame Lavoisier. “Nothing is Lost”: The Reign of Terror may have claimed Antoine Lavoisier’s life, but his revolutionary contribution...
Cornell events updated every day. Powered by the Localist Community Event Platform
urls.page_title: Virtual Info Session: Humanities Scholars Program 2025-26 Postdoctoral Associate Positions - Cornell
urls.page_summary: The Humanities Scholars Program, an undergraduate research initiative in the College of Arts & Sciences, invites applications from Cornell PhD candidates and recent Cornell PhDs for one-year postdoctoral associate positions beginning August 1, 202...
urls.page_title: The College of Arts & Sciences, Cornell University
urls.page_summary: Liberal arts college at Cornell University. A home for exploration, discovery and imagination. Undergraduate, faculty excellence in research and education. Academic diversity in the humanities, biological sciences and discipline-based education.
urls.page_title: Survival of the luckiest? New study hints at the potential role of luck in evolution : Shots - Health News : NPR
urls.page_summary: A new study on mice shows that luck in early life can determine success as an adult, especially when competition is high.

Matthew Zipple is a Ph.D. candidate in biology at Duke University. His research explores how social relationships and behavior influence offspring survival, focusing on primates. At Cornell, he will build and test a model which connects maternal s...
